notes: Minimal STRUCTURAL placeholder seeded at Author from rm-captions' outputs (captions.json + CaptionOverlay.tsx). Charter refines this into prompt-driven evals with a media-judge vision dimension (caption legibility, ±150ms sync, safe-zone). Do not hand-code regex here.

expectations.desc: scripts/timing-to-captions.mjs reads a 04-timing.json and writes a Caption[] JSON whose entries have integer startMs/endMs (milliseconds, not seconds) with endMs > startMs, a timestampMs, confidence:null, and a leading-space text token; the track is monotonic.

CaptionOverlay.tsx exists at remotion-template/src/components/ (and, post-init.sh, at remotion-project/src/components/) and imports createTikTokStyleCaptions plus the Caption and TikTokPage types from @remotion/captions.

CaptionOverlay is frame-driven only: no CSS transition/@keyframes/animate-*, no setTimeout/setInterval/Date.now/Math.random; every interpolate() passes extrapolateLeft+extrapolateRight clamp; captions render inside the lower safe-zone band.

when 04-timing.json has an empty words[] (timing_method 'estimated'), the converter writes an empty Caption[] and warns rather than inventing per-word timings (the caller then falls back to beat-level captions or skips captions).
